question: Q.1 An extreme or irrational fear of heights.
explanation: Acrophobia (acro=high) is fear of heights. Agoraphobia is fear of open spaces; Claustrophobia is fear of small spaces; Hydrophobia is fear of water.

question: Q.2 A collection of dried plant specimens.
explanation: Herbarium is for dried plants. Arboretum is for living trees; Portfolio is a work collection; Larder is for food storage.

question: Q.3 An obsessive desire to set fire to things.
explanation: Pyromania (pyro=fire) is the urge to set fire. Kleptomania is the urge to steal; Bibliomania is for books; Dipsomania is for alcohol.

question: Q.4 A solution or remedy for all diseases.
explanation: Panacea is a cure-all. Inimitable cannot be copied; Portfolio is a case for papers; Scullery is a dish-washing room.

question: Q.5 Trees that shed their leaves annually.
explanation: Deciduous trees lose leaves yearly. Fauna is animal life; Flora is plant life; Arboretum is a tree garden.

question: Q.6 Fear of being enclosed in a small, confined space.
explanation: Claustrophobia is fear of small spaces. Acrophobia is heights; Aerophobia is air/flying; Zoophobia is animals.

question: Q.7 A recurrent urge to steal without economic motive.
explanation: Kleptomania is the urge to steal. Pyromania is fire; Dipsomania is alcohol; Bibliomania is books.

question: Q.8 A wild animal's well-hidden resting place.
explanation: Lair/Den is for wild animals. Hutch is for rabbits; Stable for horses; Kennel for dogs.

question: Q.9 Fear of water.
explanation: Hydrophobia (hydro=water) is fear of water. Pyrophobia is fire; Zoophobia is animals; Aerophobia is air.

question: Q.10 A room or large cupboard for storing food.
explanation: Larder is for food. Scullery is for washing dishes; Wardrobe is for clothes; Archives are for records.

question: Q.11 A passionate enthusiasm for collecting books.
explanation: Bibliomania (biblio=book) is for books. Kleptomania is stealing; Dipsomania is alcohol; Pyromania is fire.

question: Q.12 Impossible to harm or damage.
explanation: Invulnerable cannot be harmed. Insoluble cannot be dissolved; Inimitable cannot be copied; Infallible cannot make mistakes.

question: Q.13 A glass tank where fish or aquatic plants are kept.
explanation: Aquarium is for fish. Reservoir is a large water supply lake; Apiary is for bees; Aviary is for birds.

question: Q.14 A botanical garden devoted to trees.
explanation: Arboretum (arbor=tree) is a tree garden. Herbarium is for dried plants; Granary stores grain; Hangar houses planes.

question: Q.15 So good or unusual as to be impossible to copy.
explanation: Inimitable cannot be imitated. Invulnerable cannot be harmed; Insoluble cannot be solved; Inevitable cannot be avoided.

question: Q.16 Fear of open spaces or crowded places.
explanation: Agoraphobia is fear of public/open spaces. Acrophobia is heights; Claustrophobia is small spaces; Pyrophobia is fire.

question: Q.17 An uncontrollable craving for alcoholic liquors.
explanation: Dipsomania is craving for alcohol. Kleptomania is stealing; Bibliomania is books; Pyromania is fire.

question: Q.18 A hiding place for ammunition, food, or treasures.
explanation: Caché is a secret hiding place. Arsenal stores weapons; Archives store records; Mint makes coins.

question: Q.19 The animals of a particular region.
explanation: Fauna refers to animals. Flora refers to plants; Deciduous is trees that shed leaves; Amphibians live on land and water.

question: Q.20 A box or cage for keeping rabbits.
explanation: Hutch is for rabbits. Kennel is for dogs; Stable is for horses; Apiary is for bees.

question: Q.21 Fear of animals.
explanation: Zoophobia (zoo=animal) is fear of animals. Hydrophobia is water; Pyrophobia is fire; Aerophobia is air.

question: Q.22 A large tall cupboard for storing clothes.
explanation: Wardrobe is for clothes. Larder is for food; Scullery is for washing; Dormitory is for sleeping.

question: Q.23 Impossible to solve or dissolve.
explanation: Insoluble cannot be solved/dissolved. Invisible cannot be seen; Inaudible cannot be heard; Inedible cannot be eaten.

question: Q.24 A small room at the back of a house used for washing dishes.
explanation: Scullery is for washing dishes. Larder is for food; Dormitory is for sleeping; Sanatorium is a health station.

question: Q.25 Fear of fire.
explanation: Pyrophobia (pyro=fire) is fear of fire. Hydrophobia is water; Zoophobia is animals; Acrophobia is heights.

question: Q.26 A large natural or artificial lake used as water supply.
explanation: Reservoir is a water supply lake. Aquarium is for fish; Aviary is for birds; Archives are for records.

question: Q.27 A thin flat case for loose papers or work collection.
explanation: Portfolio is a work collection. Wardrobe is for clothes; Larder for food; Archives for records.

question: Q.28 The plants of a particular region.
explanation: Flora refers to plants. Fauna refers to animals; Deciduous is leaf-shedding; Herbarium is dried plants.

question: Q.29 A part of a city occupied by a minority group.
explanation: Ghetto is a minority neighborhood. Asylum is for the ill; Sanatorium for chronic care; Dormitory is for sleeping.

question: Q.30 Fear of air or drafts of air.
explanation: Aerophobia (aero=air) is fear of air/flying. Hydrophobia is water; Acrophobia is heights; Zoophobia is animals.
